basics.name: Andrew G. Grossman, MSc, MBA
basics.summary: Seasoned developer and engineering lead with 20+ years of experience, including a background in commercial data collection and processing. Strong background in PostgreSQL, Python, and Django, with experience in AWS, GCP, Kubernetes, ETL, optimization, and software development management.

work.name: Bestow
work.summary: Staff Software Engineer on the Enrollment Platform team from February 2025; promoted to Engineering Manager of the Enrollment Platform Core team in January 2026, leading a Python/Flask platform team on GCP/Kubernetes.
highlights: Enabled multi-actor insurance applications (owner ≠ insured) through cross-team coordination, delivering juvenile products for Transamerica and USAA including a live indexed universal life product.

Helped establish a new external data service integration framework, building the attending physician statements integration and directing team members through subsequent integrations for medical and prescription data.

Maintained and extended core platform capabilities across REST APIs, automated underwriting configuration (CUE), and event-driven service integrations (Pub/Sub, gRPC, Protobufs); performed releases and served as on-call engineer and incident commander.
Scoped a release tooling initiative to stabilize the team's deployment process and drove execution through three Central Europe contractors.
Served as hiring manager for two roles (contractor and FTE); also conducted technical interviews across additional hiring pipelines.
Served as an informal onboarding anchor for incoming engineers on a complex platform, anticipating knowledge gaps and reducing ramp-up friction as the division scaled from ~10 to 30+ engineers.
Championed AI tooling adoption (Claude, Claude Code, Gemini, Cursor) and participated in the company AI working group.

work.name: Cabinet Health
work.summary: Backend engineer for Cabinet's online pharmacy offering. Implemented software features in a Python/Django-based system supporting the customer account portal, internal business management systems, and integrations with pharmacy partners.
highlights: Designed and optimized API endpoints to handle 10M+ provider and pharmacy records for prescription acquisition, reducing refill response times by 30%. Cleaned underlying third-party data using Polars.
Implemented an automated notification system to send timely SMS and email alerts, increasing refill rates by 20% and optimizing operational workload by 15%.

work.name: Songtrust / Downtown Music Publishing
work.summary: Engineering lead for the Songtrust product team. Responsible for the development and maintenance of the Songtrust.com Django/PostgreSQL web application with 30k+ active users, as well as internal facing enterprise tools. Managed a team of 6.
highlights: Completed initiative to verify user identities, reactivating stalled business partnership impacting $2M of client earnings.
Implemented SLA for incoming support requests, ensuring 95% of requests are responded to within 24 hours. Cleared 9 month request backlog in 3 months.
Identified $35K/year of since-realized savings in cloud costs.
Applied more comprehensive monitoring and alerting, identifying ~75% of user-facing issues before they were reported.

Functioned as departmental service owner for Postgres. Managed AWS RDS Postgres instances, including right-sizing, cost management, major version upgrades, and parameter configuration. Mentored new departmental DBA.

work.name: Songtrust / Downtown Music Publishing
work.summary: Joined as Senior Software Engineer; promoted to Engineering Manager in 2020. Led a greenfield Django/DRF/PostgreSQL application managing music industry intellectual properties for royalty collection. Managed and mentored 9 engineers across multiple development teams.
highlights: Drove approach to reduce data record rejections from business partners by 95% versus the previous system.
Designed and implemented a custom ETL pipeline using Python and SQL for data ingress from multiple systems, including MS SQL Server sourced data.
Architected data model, including support for robust auditability.
Maintained application deployment environments (AWS EC2/RDS Postgres/S3).

work.name: Bridgestone Commercial Solutions
work.summary: Led a team of of 8 developers to build a commercial data collection product, that served as a key differentiator in the retread service package offering to hundreds of B2B customers. Product was built using a Django/Dojo/PostgreSQL/RHEL stack. At time of departure, the application had over 600 users and had collected over 3 million survey readings.
highlights: Architected and implemented application foundation using Django/Tastypie/Dojo frameworks.
Engineered third-party data imports (ETL) from various formats into PostgreSQL database.

work.name: Bridgestone Commercial Solutions
work.summary: Developed and administered a Django-based commercial website for the tracking of national trucking fleet inventories. Resulting website was marketed directly to large trucking fleets including Waste Management, Ryder, and UPS, driving increased sales of the company's core product, and remains in active use as of 2026. Product was built using a Django/YUI 2/PostgreSQL/RHEL stack. System consolidated data from over 30 Postgres-based systems into a single reporting database.

prunedWork.name: Notes Frequencies, Inc.
prunedWork.summary: Backend architect for Notes' financial management platform for musicians.
highlights: Architected greenfield application based on Django/Django-Ninja/PostgreSQL/Celery/Docker/AWS.
Automated processing and loading of financial statements using Pandas.
Automated account registration and website/API data retrieval activities using Python and Selenium.

prunedWork.name: Stifel Nicolaus
prunedWork.summary: Developed advanced Microsoft Excel applications w/VBA for stock selection and tracking.

education.area: Master of Science in Computer Science
Bachelor of Science in Computer Science
